reasoning.evidence: No mention of Nablarchバッチ, BatchAction, DataReader, or batch-related processing. Content is entirely focused on web session management.
No mention of Jakarta Batch, JSR 352, jBatch, Batchlet, or Chunk processing.
No mention of RESTful, JAX-RS, @Produces, @Consumes, or JaxRsMethodBinder. Session cookies and HTTPセッション are web application concerns, not REST API concerns.
No mention of HTTPメッセージング, HTTP受信, メッセージ同期応答, or HttpMessagingRequestParsingHandler.

Official doc URL is under /handlers/web/. Module is nablarch-fw-web. Content explicitly mentions サーブレットフォワード, web.xml, HTTPセッション, セッションクッキー, forwarding_handler, and http_response_handler — all web application constructs. The handler manages session variables in a web request/response lifecycle.
No mention of MOMメッセージング, MQ, キュー, 非同期メッセージ, or MomMessagingAction.
No mention of DB連携メッセージング, テーブルキュー, or DatabaseRecordReader. The DB usage in content (DbManagedExpiration, DbStore) is for storing session data, not for DB-based messaging.
